message:
fix(storage): make list-default ordering deterministic and route it through SQL

Two interlocking changes that make `br list` (and any code path
calling list_issues with default filters) emit a stable, repeatable
order without needing a Rust-side re-sort.

1) Tertiary sort key on `id`. The existing SQL ORDER BY for list-
   default queries used (priority, created_at). On the rare but
   real ties — same priority, same created_at down to the
   millisecond — SQLite returned rows in indeterminate physical
   order, so consecutive `br list` runs against the same store
   could shuffle a few neighbours. Added `id ASC` as the tertiary
   key to both branches (forward and `reverse=true`). Issue ids
   are unique, so this fully orders the result set.

2) Disable the Rust post-sort fast path with `&& false`. The
   helper should_sort_list_default_in_rust() previously returned
   true for the default-filter / no-offset / no-limit case so the
   storage layer could fetch unsorted and let
   sort_list_default() sort in process. Now that the SQL
   ordering is deterministic and indexed (priority and created_at
   already have indexes; id is the primary key), letting SQLite
   sort on its own is consistently faster and avoids the double
   sort on result sets large enough to spill the in-memory cache.

   Kept the original conditions in place and tacked `&& false`
   onto the end rather than inlining `false` as the function body.
   This way a future reader still sees exactly which filter
   shapes were considered "fast-path eligible", and re-enabling
   the path (e.g., to reproduce a regression or compare timings)
   is a one-character change.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
